findings.problem: Narration repeatedly uses clipped negation fragments (e.g., "Not buzzed.", "Not surrender.", "Not gone.") that read as elliptical clipping and recur as a visible prose tic. This violates the constitution's anti-clipping rule and flattens sentence texture through overused formula.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped fragment beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:411, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:457 as grammatically complete sentences with explicit subject+verb while preserving pace and pressure.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised narration at lines 127, 183, 411, and 457 contains no standalone "Not ..." fragment sentences and each line-level beat remains tense but syntactically complete; FAIL if any clipped narration fragment remains in those spans.

findings.problem: The live-stream confrontation runs for 10+ consecutive short paragraphs with minimal Ina interior access, creating screenplay drift in a chapter that otherwise promises close-third psychological pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the confrontation stretch from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:281 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:305, add interior beats (body sensation, tactical thought, or self-assessment) between dialogue turns so reader access to Ina does not disappear for long runs.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if lines 281-305 include at least three discrete Ina interior beats inserted between spoken exchanges and no run exceeds six consecutive paragraphs without interiority; FAIL if the passage remains primarily external dialogue/action for 10+ consecutive paragraphs.

findings.problem: After an explicit suicide line ("Then I find a bridge"), the scene pivots quickly into legal leverage explanation and vow rhetoric, softening immediate crisis consequence. This reads as sanitization/timidity: tension is managed conceptually instead of being rendered through concrete containment actions.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the sequence across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:325, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:337, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:349 so immediate safety management is staged on-page before contract-clause strategy resumes.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, before legal clause discussion resumes, the revised scene shows at least two concrete immediate safety actions and one explicit short-horizon survival commitment in direct speech from Ji-eun; FAIL if the chapter returns to legal strategy without those on-page containment beats.

findings.problem: Ina's climactic promise shifts into ceremonially uncontracted legal recital ("I promise I will... I do not..."), which drifts from her high-contraction private register and introduces stilted formality at peak scene pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the promise block at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:401 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:405 to keep legal clarity but use Ina's stressed natural speech rhythm (contractions, blunt concrete phrasing, fewer recital-style repeats).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Ina's revised promise uses contractions in at least 3 of 5 declarative clauses while still naming one concrete enforceable action (e.g., debt challenge or collection block); FAIL if the block remains predominantly uncontracted ceremonial diction.

findings.problem: Kwon delivers multiple control lines in this chapter but functions as a pure command channel with little individualized pressure logic, reducing antagonist dimensionality at the chapter's hinge.
findings.rewrite_direction: Deepen one Kwon communication beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:191 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:427 with a specific timed consequence and character-specific inevitability framing rather than generic institutional threat wording.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if one revised Kwon message/voicemail includes a concrete consequence with a clear timeframe and idiolect that is recognizably his (calm inevitability frame) rather than interchangeable command boilerplate; FAIL if Kwon remains functionally generic in all chapter communications.

summary: Required chapter-10 beats land (train humor, debt specificity, refusal to sedate, binding promise), continuity and canonical tense are intact, and no direct continuity-sheet contradiction was found. The chapter still fails on craft/prose rigor due repeated clipped narration formulas, an under-rendered post-suicidality containment beat (sanitization/timidity risk), interiority drop in a long confrontation run, and dialogue-register drift at the promise climax.
